Implements a music application for Pebble firmware, handling playback controls, UI updates, and event management.

# Purpose
The code is a C implementation of a music application for a Pebble smartwatch. It provides functionality to control music playback, display track information, and manage user interactions through the watch's interface. The application includes various components such as window management, UI elements, and event handling to facilitate music control and display.

Key components include the `MusicAppData` structure, which holds the state and UI elements of the application, and the `MusicAppSizeConfig` structure, which defines layout configurations for different display sizes. The code manages user interactions through button click handlers and animations for UI transitions. It also handles music events such as track changes and playback state updates, using functions like [`prv_update_now_playing`](<#prv_update_now_playing>) and [`prv_update_ui_state`](<#prv_update_ui_state>). The application interfaces with the Pebble system through event subscriptions and uses resources like icons and fonts to render the UI. The main function initializes the application, sets up event handling, and enters the event loop to respond to user actions and system events.

# Global Variables

---
### s\_music\_size\_config\_medium
- **Type**: ``MusicAppSizeConfig``
- **Description**: Defines the layout and style configuration for a medium-sized music app interface. It includes font keys, margins, and dimensions for various UI elements such as artist, title, and time fields, as well as cassette animation properties.
- **Use**: Used to configure the appearance and layout of the music app interface for medium-sized displays.


---
### s\_music\_size\_config\_large
- **Type**: ``MusicAppSizeConfig``
- **Description**: Defines the layout and style configuration for a large music app interface. It includes font keys for music time and no music text, horizontal margins, and dimensions and positions for various UI elements such as artist, title, and time fields, as well as cassette animation properties.
- **Use**: Used to configure the appearance and layout of the music app interface for large display sizes.


---
### s\_music\_size\_configs
- **Type**: ``MusicAppSizeConfig * const` array`
- **Description**: An array of pointers to `MusicAppSizeConfig` structures, each representing configuration settings for different preferred content sizes in a music application. The array is indexed by `NumPreferredContentSizes` and maps specific content size preferences to their corresponding configuration structures.
- **Use**: Used to select the appropriate `MusicAppSizeConfig` based on the preferred content size for the music application interface.


---
### BUTTON\_FORWARD
- **Type**: ``ButtonId``
- **Description**: Represents the button identifier for the forward button action in the music application. It is set to `BUTTON_ID_DOWN`, indicating that the down button on the device is used for the forward action.
- **Use**: Used to configure the action bar layer to handle forward button actions in the music application.


---
### BUTTON\_BACKWARD
- **Type**: ``ButtonId``
- **Description**: Represents a constant button identifier for the backward button in the music application. It is set to `BUTTON_ID_UP`, indicating that the upward button on the device is used for the backward action.
- **Use**: Used to map the upward button to the backward action in the music application.


---
### BOUNCEBACK\_OFFSET
- **Type**: ``int16_t``
- **Description**: Represents an offset value used to eliminate empty space above the first line of text in animations. This offset helps in achieving a cleaner visual presentation by clipping the text immediately at the top.
- **Use**: Used in animations to adjust the vertical position of text layers, ensuring that the text starts at the correct position without unnecessary empty space.


---
### TITLE\_BOUNDS\_OFFSET
- **Type**: ``int16_t``
- **Description**: Defines the vertical offset for the title text layer in the music application. This offset helps to remove empty space above the first line of text, allowing for neater animations by clipping immediately at the top of the text.
- **Use**: Used to adjust the vertical position of the title text layer for animation purposes.


---
### ARTIST\_BOUNDS\_OFFSET
- **Type**: ``int16_t``
- **Description**: Defines a constant integer value used to offset the bounds of the artist text layer in the user interface. This offset helps to adjust the vertical position of the artist text for better alignment and presentation.
- **Use**: Used in animations to adjust the vertical position of the artist text layer.


---
### TIME\_BOUNDS\_OFFSET
- **Type**: ``int16_t``
- **Description**: A constant integer value that represents an offset for time bounds in the application.
- **Use**: Used to adjust the position of time-related text layers in animations.


---
### VOLUME\_REPEAT\_INTERVAL\_MS
- **Type**: ``uint32_t``
- **Description**: Defines the interval in milliseconds for repeating volume adjustment actions.
- **Use**: Used to set the delay between repeated volume change commands when a volume button is held down.


---
### ACTION\_BAR\_TIMEOUT\_MS
- **Type**: ``uint32_t``
- **Description**: A constant variable that defines the timeout duration for the action bar in milliseconds.
- **Use**: Used to specify the duration after which the action bar will revert to its default state if no interaction occurs.


---
### VOLUME\_ICON\_TIMEOUT\_MS
- **Type**: ``uint32_t``
- **Description**: Defines the timeout duration for the volume icon display in milliseconds.
- **Use**: Used to specify how long the volume icon remains visible after a volume change event.


---
### s\_frame\_layer\_implementation
- **Type**: ``PropertyAnimationImplementation``
- **Description**: Defines the implementation details for a property animation that manipulates the frame of a layer using `GRect` values. It specifies the update function and the getter and setter accessors for the animation.
- **Use**: Used to create animations that update the frame of a layer by setting and getting `GRect` values.


# Data Structures

---
### ActionBarState
- **Type**: ``enum``
- **Members**:
    - ``ActionBarStateSkip``: Represents the state where the action bar is set to skip tracks.
    - ``ActionBarStateVolume``: Represents the state where the action bar is set to adjust the volume.
    - ``ActionBarStateLongPress``: Represents the state where the action bar is set for a long press action.
- **Description**: Defines the possible states of an action bar in a music application, which include skipping tracks, adjusting volume, and handling long press actions.


---
### MusicAppSizeConfig
- **Type**: ``struct``
- **Members**:
    - ``music_time_font_key``: A pointer to a string that specifies the font key for music time display.
    - ``no_music_font_key``: A pointer to a string that specifies the font key for the 'no music' display.
    - ``horizontal_margin``: An integer that defines the horizontal margin for layout.
    - ``artist_field``: A `GRangeVertical` structure that defines the vertical range for the artist field.
    - ``title_field``: A `GRangeVertical` structure that defines the vertical range for the title field.
    - ``time_field``: A `GRangeVertical` structure that defines the vertical range for the time field.
    - ``cassette_rect``: A `GRect` structure that defines the rectangle for the cassette display.
    - ``cassette_animation_x``: An integer that specifies the x-coordinate for cassette animation.
    - ``cassette_animation_time``: An integer that specifies the duration of the cassette animation in milliseconds.
    - ``track_field``: A `GRangeVertical` structure that defines the vertical range for the track field.
    - ``track_corner_radius``: An integer that specifies the corner radius for the track field.
    - ``no_music_img_pos``: A `GPoint` structure that specifies the position of the 'no music' image.
    - ``no_music_text_field``: A `GRangeVertical` structure that defines the vertical range for the 'no music' text field.
- **Description**: Defines the layout and animation configuration for a music application interface, including font keys, margins, and dimensions for various UI elements such as artist, title, time fields, and cassette animations.


---
### MusicNoMusicWindow
- **Type**: ``struct``
- **Members**:
    - ``window``: A `Window` object that represents the main window of the `MusicNoMusicWindow`.
    - ``bitmap_layer``: A `BitmapLayer` object that displays a bitmap image in the window.
    - ``bitmap``: A `GBitmap` object that holds the bitmap image data for the `bitmap_layer`.
    - ``text_layer``: A `TextLayer` object that displays text in the window.
- **Description**: Defines a window structure for a music application that displays a bitmap and text when no music is playing. It includes a `Window` for the main interface, a `BitmapLayer` for displaying images, a `GBitmap` for image data, and a `TextLayer` for displaying text messages.


---
### MusicAppData
- **Type**: ``struct``
- **Members**:
    - ``window``: Holds the main application window.
    - ``action_bar``: Manages the action bar layer for user interactions.
    - ``artist_text_layer``: Displays the artist's name.
    - ``artist_buffer``: Stores the artist's name as a string.
    - ``title_text_layer``: Displays the music track title.
    - ``title_buffer``: Stores the music track title as a string.
    - ``status_layer``: Displays the status bar layer.
    - ``position_text_layer``: Displays the current position of the track.
    - ``position_buffer``: Stores the current track position as a string.
    - ``length_text_layer``: Displays the total length of the track.
    - ``length_buffer``: Stores the total track length as a string.
    - ``transition``: Handles animations for UI transitions.
    - ``volume_icon_timer``: Manages the timer for volume icon display.
    - ``current_play_state``: Stores the current playback state of the music.
    - ``icon_skip_forward``: Holds the bitmap for the skip forward icon.
    - ``icon_skip_backward``: Holds the bitmap for the skip backward icon.
    - ``icon_ellipsis``: Holds the bitmap for the ellipsis icon.
    - ``icon_pause``: Holds the bitmap for the pause icon.
    - ``icon_play``: Holds the bitmap for the play icon.
    - ``icon_play_pause``: Holds the bitmap for the play/pause icon.
    - ``icon_volume_up``: Holds the bitmap for the volume up icon.
    - ``icon_volume_down``: Holds the bitmap for the volume down icon.
    - ``image_cassette``: Holds the bitmap for the cassette image.
    - ``image_pause``: Holds the bitmap for the pause image.
    - ``image_volume_up``: Holds the bitmap for the volume up image.
    - ``image_volume_down``: Holds the bitmap for the volume down image.
    - ``cassette_container``: Contains the layer for the cassette image.
    - ``cassette_layer``: Manages the bitmap layer for the cassette image.
    - ``cassette_current_icon``: Points to the current cassette icon bitmap.
    - ``event_info``: Stores event service information.
    - ``track_pos_bar``: Displays the progress bar for the track position.
    - ``track_length``: Stores the total length of the track in milliseconds.
    - ``track_pos``: Stores the current position of the track in milliseconds.
    - ``pause_track_pos_updates``: Indicates if track position updates are paused.
    - ``action_bar_state``: Stores the current state of the action bar.
    - ``action_bar_revert_timer``: Manages the timer for reverting the action bar state.
    - ``volume_repeat_timer``: Manages the timer for repeating volume changes.
    - ``volume_is_up``: Indicates if the volume is increasing.
    - ``no_music_window``: Points to the window displayed when no music is playing.
    - ``score``: Points to the vibration score, if supported.
- **Description**: Manages the user interface and state for a music application, including display elements like text layers for artist and title, bitmaps for icons, and layers for progress and status. It also handles animations, timers, and event services to provide a responsive and interactive music playback experience. The structure supports various playback states and user interactions through an action bar and other UI components.

[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/music_app.c#L157>)

- **Logic and Control Flow**:

- **Output**: An integer representing the width available for content display.

### prv\_show\_volume\_image<!-- {{#callable:prv_show_volume_image}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/music_app.c#L294>)

Updates the volume icon display and manages the timer for its visibility.
- **Inputs**:
    - `bitmap`: A pointer to a `GBitmap` that represents the volume icon to be displayed.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Retrieves user data from the application state using `app_state_get_user_data()`.
    - Checks if a timer for the volume icon is already set.
    - If the timer is set, it reschedules the timer with a new timeout value.
    - If the timer is not set, it registers a new timer that will call `prv_handle_volume_icon_timer` after a specified timeout.
    - Calls [`prv_trigger_cassette_icon_switch`](<#prv_trigger_cassette_icon_switch>) to update the display with the new volume icon.
- **Output**: No return value; the function modifies the state of the volume icon display and manages the timer.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`prv_trigger_cassette_icon_switch`](<#prv_trigger_cassette_icon_switch>)

### prv\_change\_volume<!-- {{#callable:prv_change_volume}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/music_app.c#L305>)

Changes the volume of the music application based on the input flag.
- **Inputs**:
    - `volume_is_up`: A boolean flag indicating whether to increase or decrease the volume.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Checks if the volume up and down commands are supported using [`music_is_command_supported`](<../../services/normal/music.c.md#music_is_command_supported>).
    - If either command is not supported, the function returns early.
    - Retrieves user data from the application state using `app_state_get_user_data`.
    - Displays the appropriate volume image based on the value of `volume_is_up` using [`prv_show_volume_image`](<#prv_show_volume_image>).
    - Sends the corresponding volume command using [`music_command_send`](<../../services/normal/music.c.md#music_command_send>).
- **Output**: No return value; the function performs actions to change the volume and update the UI.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`music_is_command_supported`](<../../services/normal/music.c.md#music_is_command_supported>)
    - [`prv_show_volume_image`](<#prv_show_volume_image>)
    - [`music_command_send`](<../../services/normal/music.c.md#music_command_send>)

### prv\_create\_layer\_upwards\_animation<!-- {{#callable:prv_create_layer_upwards_animation}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/music_app.c#L316>)

Creates an upwards animation for a specified `Layer`.
- **Inputs**:
    - `layer`: A pointer to the `Layer` that will be animated.
    - `offset`: An integer value that specifies the vertical offset for the animation.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calculates the target position for the animation based on the `Layer`'s height and the provided `offset`.
    - Creates an `Animation` object using [`property_animation_create_bounds_origin`](<../../applib/ui/property_animation.c.md#property_animation_create_bounds_origin>) with the calculated origin and target points.
    - Sets the duration of the animation to 4 times the frame duration defined by `ANIMATION_FRAME_MS`.
    - Applies an easing curve to the animation using `AnimationCurveEaseIn`.
- **Output**: Returns a pointer to the created `Animation` object.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`GPoint`](<../../applib/graphics/gtypes.h.md#GPoint>)
    - [`property_animation_get_animation`](<../../applib/ui/property_animation.c.md#property_animation_get_animation>)
    - [`property_animation_create_bounds_origin`](<../../applib/ui/property_animation.c.md#property_animation_create_bounds_origin>)

- **Functions Called**:

    - [`animation_spawn_create`](<../../applib/ui/animation.c.md#animation_spawn_create>)
    - [`prv_create_layer_bounceback_animation`](<#prv_create_layer_bounceback_animation>)
    - [`prv_artist_rect`](<#prv_artist_rect>)
    - [`prv_title_rect`](<#prv_title_rect>)

### prv\_flip\_animated\_text<!-- {{#callable:prv_flip_animated_text}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/music_app.c#L367>)

Restores the bounds of text layers and updates the track progress.
- **Inputs**:
    - `animation`: Pointer to an `Animation` structure that contains animation data.
    - `finished`: Boolean indicating if the animation has finished.
    - `context`: Pointer to a `void` that holds user data, specifically a pointer to `MusicAppData`.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Sets the `pause_track_pos_updates` field of `MusicAppData` to false.
    - Calls [`prv_update_track_progress`](<#prv_update_track_progress>) to refresh the track progress display.
    - Calls `music_get_now_playing` to update the title and artist buffers.
    - Restores the vertical bounds of the title, artist, position, and length text layers to their original positions.
- **Output**: The function does not return a value; it modifies the state of the `MusicAppData` and updates the UI.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`prv_update_track_progress`](<#prv_update_track_progress>)


---
### prv\_should\_animate\_casssette<!-- {{#callable:prv_should_animate_casssette}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/music_app.c#L379>)

Determines if the cassette animation should play based on the music playback state.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calls `music_get_playback_state()` to retrieve the current playback state.
    - Compares the playback state to `MusicPlayStatePaused`.
- **Output**: Returns `true` if the playback state is not paused, otherwise returns `false`.


---
### prv\_create\_cassette\_animation<!-- {{#callable:prv_create_cassette_animation}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/music_app.c#L383>)

Creates a sequence of animations for a cassette UI element.
- **Inputs**:
    - `data`: Pointer to a `MusicAppData` structure containing UI and state information.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Retrieves the size configuration using `prv_config()`.
    - Calculates the rectangle for the cassette using `prv_cassette_rect()`.
    - Defines target points for the cassette's left and right animations.
    - Creates three animations: moving left, moving right, and bouncing back.
    - Sets the duration and curve for each animation.
    - Creates a sequence of the three animations.
    - Checks if the cassette should animate using `prv_should_animate_casssette()`, and sets the play count accordingly.
- **Output**: Returns a pointer to the created animation sequence.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`prv_config`](<#prv_config>)
    - [`prv_cassette_rect`](<#prv_cassette_rect>)
    - [`GPoint`](<../../applib/graphics/gtypes.h.md#GPoint>)
    - [`property_animation_get_animation`](<../../applib/ui/property_animation.c.md#property_animation_get_animation>)
    - [`property_animation_create_bounds_origin`](<../../applib/ui/property_animation.c.md#property_animation_create_bounds_origin>)
    - [`animation_sequence_create`](<../../applib/ui/animation.c.md#animation_sequence_create>)
    - [`prv_should_animate_casssette`](<#prv_should_animate_casssette>)


---
### prv\_trigger\_track\_change\_animation<!-- {{#callable:prv_trigger_track_change_animation}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/music_app.c#L410>)

Triggers the animation for changing tracks in the music app.
- **Inputs**:
    - `data`: Pointer to a `MusicAppData` structure that holds the state and UI elements of the music application.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Checks if an animation is already scheduled using `animation_is_scheduled`.
    - If an animation is scheduled, the function returns early.
    - Sets `pause_track_pos_updates` to true to stop track position updates during the animation.
    - Creates an upwards animation using [`prv_create_upwards_animation`](<#prv_create_upwards_animation>).
    - Creates a bounceback animation using [`prv_create_bounceback_animation`](<#prv_create_bounceback_animation>).
    - Sets the handler for the upwards animation to call `prv_flip_animated_text` when it stops.
    - Creates a sequence of animations combining the upwards and bounceback animations.
    - Stores the created animation sequence in `data->transition`.
    - Schedules the animation sequence using `animation_schedule`.
- **Output**: No return value; the function schedules an animation sequence for the UI.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`prv_create_upwards_animation`](<#prv_create_upwards_animation>)
    - [`prv_create_bounceback_animation`](<#prv_create_bounceback_animation>)
    - [`animation_sequence_create`](<../../applib/ui/animation.c.md#animation_sequence_create>)


---
### prv\_update\_icon<!-- {{#callable:prv_update_icon}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/music_app.c#L439>)

Updates the cassette icon in the music app.
- **Inputs**:
    - `animation`: Pointer to an `Animation` structure that contains animation details.
    - `finished`: Boolean indicating if the animation has finished.
    - `context`: Pointer to a `GBitmap` that represents the new icon to be displayed.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Retrieves user data from the application state using `app_state_get_user_data()`.
    - Sets the bitmap of the `cassette_layer` to the new bitmap provided in the `context`.
    - Resets the y-coordinate of the `cassette_layer` bounds to 0.
- **Output**: The function does not return a value; it updates the UI directly.


---
### prv\_trigger\_cassette\_icon\_switch<!-- {{#callable:prv_trigger_cassette_icon_switch}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/music_app.c#L446>)

Switches the cassette icon in the music app with an optional animation.
- **Inputs**:
    - `new_bitmap`: A pointer to a `GBitmap` that represents the new icon to display.
    - `animated`: A boolean that indicates whether the icon switch should be animated.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Retrieves the current user data from the application state.
    - If `animated` is false, sets the new bitmap directly and updates the current icon.
    - Checks if the new bitmap is the same as the current icon; if so, exits without action.
    - Calculates the rectangle for the cassette icon.
    - Creates an animation to make the current icon disappear.
    - Creates a bounce-back animation for the new icon.
    - Sets a handler to update the icon after the disappear animation completes.
    - Creates a sequence of animations and schedules it.
- **Output**: The function does not return a value but updates the UI to reflect the new cassette icon.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`prv_cassette_rect`](<#prv_cassette_rect>)
    - [`property_animation_get_animation`](<../../applib/ui/property_animation.c.md#property_animation_get_animation>)
    - [`property_animation_create_bounds_origin`](<../../applib/ui/property_animation.c.md#property_animation_create_bounds_origin>)
    - [`GPoint`](<../../applib/graphics/gtypes.h.md#GPoint>)
    - [`property_animation_create`](<../../applib/ui/property_animation.c.md#property_animation_create>)
    - [`animation_sequence_create`](<../../applib/ui/animation.c.md#animation_sequence_create>)


---
### prv\_update\_cassette\_icon<!-- {{#callable:prv_update_cassette_icon}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/music_app.c#L490>)

Updates the cassette icon based on the current playback state.
- **Inputs**:
    - `data`: Pointer to a `MusicAppData` structure containing the app's data.
    - `animated`: Boolean indicating whether the icon update should be animated.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Checks the current playback state using `music_get_playback_state()`.
    - If the playback state is `MusicPlayStatePaused`, calls `prv_trigger_cassette_icon_switch()` with the pause icon.
    - If the playback state is not paused, calls `prv_trigger_cassette_icon_switch()` with the cassette icon.
- **Output**: No return value; updates the cassette icon displayed in the UI.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`prv_trigger_cassette_icon_switch`](<#prv_trigger_cassette_icon_switch>)


---
### prv\_update\_ui\_state\_skipping<!-- {{#callable:prv_update_ui_state_skipping}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/music_app.c#L498>)

Updates the user interface state for skipping tracks.
- **Inputs**:
    - `data`: Pointer to a `MusicAppData` structure containing the application state.
    - `animated`: Boolean indicating whether the icon updates should be animated.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Sets the click configuration provider for the action bar to `prv_skipping_click_config_provider`.
    - Updates the action bar icons for forward and backward skipping using [`action_bar_layer_set_icon_animated`](<../../applib/ui/action_bar_layer.c.md#action_bar_layer_set_icon_animated>).
    - Checks the current playback state using `music_get_playback_state()`.
    - If the playback state is paused, sets the select button icon to the play icon; otherwise, sets it to an ellipsis icon.
- **Output**: No return value; the function modifies the UI state directly.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`action_bar_layer_set_click_config_provider`](<../../applib/ui/action_bar_layer.c.md#action_bar_layer_set_click_config_provider>)
    - [`action_bar_layer_set_icon_animated`](<../../applib/ui/action_bar_layer.c.md#action_bar_layer_set_icon_animated>)


---
### prv\_update\_ui\_state\_volume<!-- {{#callable:prv_update_ui_state_volume}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/music_app.c#L514>)

Updates the user interface state for volume controls in the music application.
- **Inputs**:
    - `data`: Pointer to a `MusicAppData` structure that holds the current state and UI elements of the music application.
    - `animated`: Boolean value indicating whether the UI updates should be animated.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Checks if the `action_bar_state` is `ActionBarStateVolume` to set the click configuration provider for volume controls.
    - Sets the icons for volume up and volume down buttons using [`action_bar_layer_set_icon_animated`](<../../applib/ui/action_bar_layer.c.md#action_bar_layer_set_icon_animated>).
    - Determines the appropriate icon for the select button based on the current playback state using a switch statement.
    - Updates the select button icon with the determined icon.
- **Output**: No return value; the function modifies the UI state directly.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`action_bar_layer_set_click_config_provider`](<../../applib/ui/action_bar_layer.c.md#action_bar_layer_set_click_config_provider>)
    - [`action_bar_layer_set_icon_animated`](<../../applib/ui/action_bar_layer.c.md#action_bar_layer_set_icon_animated>)


---
### prv\_update\_ui\_state<!-- {{#callable:prv_update_ui_state}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/music_app.c#L532>)

Updates the user interface state based on the current action bar state and playback state.
- **Inputs**:
    - `data`: Pointer to a `MusicAppData` structure containing the current state of the music application.
    - `animated`: Boolean indicating whether the UI updates should be animated.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Checks if the `action_bar_state` in `data` is `ActionBarStateSkip`.
    - If true, calls [`prv_update_ui_state_skipping`](<#prv_update_ui_state_skipping>) to update the UI for skipping state.
    - If false, calls [`prv_update_ui_state_volume`](<#prv_update_ui_state_volume>) to update the UI for volume state.
    - Retrieves the current playback state using `music_get_playback_state`.
    - If the current playback state differs from `data->current_play_state`, updates `data->current_play_state`.
    - Calls [`prv_update_cassette_icon`](<#prv_update_cassette_icon>) to update the cassette icon based on the new playback state.
- **Output**: This function does not return a value; it modifies the UI state and updates the display based on the current playback state.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`prv_update_ui_state_skipping`](<#prv_update_ui_state_skipping>)
    - [`prv_update_ui_state_volume`](<#prv_update_ui_state_volume>)
    - [`prv_update_cassette_icon`](<#prv_update_cassette_icon>)


---
### prv\_set\_action\_bar\_state<!-- {{#callable:prv_set_action_bar_state}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/music_app.c#L545>)

Sets the action bar state and updates the UI accordingly.
- **Inputs**:
    - `data`: Pointer to a `MusicAppData` structure that holds the state of the music application.
    - `state`: An enumeration value of type `ActionBarState` that indicates the new state of the action bar.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Assigns the provided `state` to the `action_bar_state` field of the `data` structure.
    - Calls [`prv_update_ui_state`](<#prv_update_ui_state>) with `data` and `true` to refresh the UI based on the new action bar state.
- **Output**: The function does not return a value.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`prv_update_ui_state`](<#prv_update_ui_state>)


---
### prv\_action\_bar\_revert<!-- {{#callable:prv_action_bar_revert}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/music_app.c#L550>)

Reverts the action bar state to 'skip' and cancels the revert timer.
- **Inputs**:
    - `context`: A pointer to `MusicAppData` structure that holds the application state.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Assigns the `context` pointer to a `MusicAppData` pointer named `data`.
    - Sets the `action_bar_revert_timer` field of `data` to NULL.
    - Calls [`prv_set_action_bar_state`](<#prv_set_action_bar_state>) with `data` and `ActionBarStateSkip`.
- **Output**: No return value; the function modifies the state of the `MusicAppData` structure.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`prv_set_action_bar_state`](<#prv_set_action_bar_state>)


---
### reset\_action\_bar\_revert\_timer<!-- {{#callable:reset_action_bar_revert_timer}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/music_app.c#L556>)

Reschedules the action bar revert timer.
- **Inputs**:
    - `data`: Pointer to a `MusicAppData` structure that contains the state of the music application.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calls `app_timer_reschedule` with the current timer and a predefined timeout value `ACTION_BAR_TIMEOUT_MS`.
- **Output**: The function does not return a value.


---
### prv\_skip\_click\_handler<!-- {{#callable:prv_skip_click_handler}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/music_app.c#L560>)

Handles click events to skip tracks in a music application.
- **Inputs**:
    - `recognizer`: A reference to the click recognizer that detects button clicks.
    - `context`: A pointer to user-defined data, typically used to pass application state.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Creates a cassette animation using [`prv_create_cassette_animation`](<#prv_create_cassette_animation>) with the provided `context`.
    - Schedules the created animation using `animation_schedule`.
    - Checks if the button pressed is `BUTTON_BACKWARD` using `click_recognizer_get_button_id`.
    - Sends a command to play the previous track if the backward button is pressed, otherwise sends a command to play the next track.
- **Output**: No return value; the function triggers animations and sends music commands based on user input.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`prv_create_cassette_animation`](<#prv_create_cassette_animation>)
    - [`music_command_send`](<../../services/normal/music.c.md#music_command_send>)


---
### prv\_volume\_click\_handler<!-- {{#callable:prv_volume_click_handler}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/music_app.c#L571>)

Handles volume button clicks to adjust volume and provide haptic feedback.
- **Inputs**:
    - `recognizer`: A reference to the click recognizer that detects button clicks.
    - `context`: A pointer to user-defined data, typically used to pass application state.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calls [`reset_action_bar_revert_timer`](<#reset_action_bar_revert_timer>) to reset the timer for the action bar.
    - Determines if the volume should increase or decrease based on the button ID from `click_recognizer_get_button_id`.
    - Calls [`prv_change_volume`](<#prv_change_volume>) to change the volume based on the button pressed.
    - Checks if the number of clicks is two or more using `click_number_of_clicks_counted` to trigger haptic feedback via [`prv_do_haptic_feedback_vibe`](<#prv_do_haptic_feedback_vibe>).
- **Output**: No return value; the function modifies the volume and may trigger haptic feedback.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`reset_action_bar_revert_timer`](<#reset_action_bar_revert_timer>)
    - [`prv_change_volume`](<#prv_change_volume>)
    - [`prv_do_haptic_feedback_vibe`](<#prv_do_haptic_feedback_vibe>)


---
### prv\_ellipsis\_click\_handler<!-- {{#callable:prv_ellipsis_click_handler}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/music_app.c#L583>)

Handles click events on the ellipsis button to set the action bar state to volume control and starts a timer for reverting the action bar.
- **Inputs**:
    - `recognizer`: A reference to the click recognizer that detects the click event.
    - `context`: A pointer to the context data, which is expected to be of type `MusicAppData`.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Retrieves the `MusicAppData` structure from the `context` pointer.
    - Registers a timer using `app_timer_register` to call `prv_action_bar_revert` after a specified timeout.
    - Calls [`prv_set_action_bar_state`](<#prv_set_action_bar_state>) to change the action bar state to `ActionBarStateVolume`.
- **Output**: No direct output; modifies the state of the action bar and sets a timer.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`prv_set_action_bar_state`](<#prv_set_action_bar_state>)


---
### prv\_toggle\_playing<!-- {{#callable:prv_toggle_playing}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/music_app.c#L590>)

Toggles the play/pause state of the music playback.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calls [`music_command_send`](<../../services/normal/music.c.md#music_command_send>) with `MusicCommandTogglePlayPause` to change the playback state.
- **Output**: No output is returned; the function performs an action to change the music playback state.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`music_command_send`](<../../services/normal/music.c.md#music_command_send>)

### prv\_play\_pause\_click\_handler<!-- {{#callable:prv_play_pause_click_handler}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/music_app.c#L594>)

Handles play/pause button clicks.
- **Inputs**:
    - `recognizer`: A reference to the click recognizer.
    - `context`: A pointer to the context data.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calls [`reset_action_bar_revert_timer`](<#reset_action_bar_revert_timer>) with `context` to reset the timer.
    - Calls [`prv_toggle_playing`](<#prv_toggle_playing>) to toggle the play/pause state.
- **Output**: No output is returned.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`reset_action_bar_revert_timer`](<#reset_action_bar_revert_timer>)
    - [`prv_toggle_playing`](<#prv_toggle_playing>)

### prv\_handle\_volume\_repeat<!-- {{#callable:prv_handle_volume_repeat}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/music_app.c#L599>)

Handles volume change repeatedly while a button is held down.
- **Inputs**:
    - `context`: A pointer to `MusicAppData` structure that holds the application state.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Checks if `volume_repeat_timer` is not set; if so, exits the function.
    - Registers a timer to call itself again after a defined interval.
    - Calls [`prv_change_volume`](<#prv_change_volume>) to adjust the volume based on the current state.
    - Calls [`prv_do_haptic_feedback_vibe`](<#prv_do_haptic_feedback_vibe>) to provide haptic feedback.
- **Output**: No return value; the function modifies the state of the application and triggers volume changes.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`prv_change_volume`](<#prv_change_volume>)
    - [`prv_do_haptic_feedback_vibe`](<#prv_do_haptic_feedback_vibe>)

### prv\_volume\_long\_click\_start\_handler<!-- {{#callable:prv_volume_long_click_start_handler}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/music_app.c#L610>)

Handles the start of a long click event for volume adjustment.
- **Inputs**:
    - `recognizer`: A reference to the click recognizer that detects button clicks.
    - `context`: A pointer to the context data, which contains the state of the music application.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Determines if the volume should be increased or decreased based on the button ID from the `recognizer`.
    - Calls [`prv_change_volume`](<#prv_change_volume>) to adjust the volume.
    - Sets the action bar state to `ActionBarStateLongPress`.
    - Updates the `volume_is_up` field in the `MusicAppData` structure.
    - Registers a timer for repeated volume changes using `app_timer_register`.
    - Triggers haptic feedback using [`prv_do_haptic_feedback_vibe`](<#prv_do_haptic_feedback_vibe>).
- **Output**: No return value; modifies the state of the music application and triggers volume changes.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`prv_change_volume`](<#prv_change_volume>)
    - [`prv_set_action_bar_state`](<#prv_set_action_bar_state>)
    - [`prv_do_haptic_feedback_vibe`](<#prv_do_haptic_feedback_vibe>)


---
### prv\_volume\_long\_click\_end\_handler<!-- {{#callable:prv_volume_long_click_end_handler}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/music_app.c#L621>)

Handles the end of a long click event for volume control.
- **Inputs**:
    - `recognizer`: A reference to the click recognizer that detects the click event.
    - `context`: A pointer to the `MusicAppData` structure containing application state.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Retrieves the `MusicAppData` structure from the `context` pointer.
    - Sets the action bar state to `ActionBarStateSkip` using [`prv_set_action_bar_state`](<#prv_set_action_bar_state>).
    - Cancels the volume repeat timer using `app_timer_cancel`.
    - Sets the volume repeat timer to `NULL`.
- **Output**: The function does not return a value. It modifies the state of the application by updating the action bar and canceling the volume repeat timer.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`prv_set_action_bar_state`](<#prv_set_action_bar_state>)

### prv\_play\_pause\_long\_click\_start\_handler<!-- {{#callable:prv_play_pause_long_click_start_handler}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/music_app.c#L628>)

Handles the start of a long click on the play/pause button.
- **Inputs**:
    - `recognizer`: A reference to the click recognizer that detects the button press.
    - `context`: A pointer to the context data associated with the click event.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calls `prv_toggle_playing()` to toggle the play/pause state.
    - Sets the action bar state to `ActionBarStateLongPress` using `prv_set_action_bar_state()`.
    - Triggers haptic feedback using `prv_do_haptic_feedback_vibe()`.
- **Output**: No return value; the function performs actions related to the play/pause state and user feedback.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`prv_toggle_playing`](<#prv_toggle_playing>)
    - [`prv_set_action_bar_state`](<#prv_set_action_bar_state>)

### prv\_play\_pause\_long\_click\_end\_handler<!-- {{#callable:prv_play_pause_long_click_end_handler}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/music_app.c#L634>)

Handles the end of a long click event for the play/pause button.
- **Inputs**:
    - `recognizer`: A reference to the click recognizer that detects the button click.
    - `context`: A pointer to the context data associated with the click event.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calls [`prv_set_action_bar_state`](<#prv_set_action_bar_state>) with the context and sets the action bar state to `ActionBarStateSkip`.
- **Output**: No return value; the function modifies the state of the action bar.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`prv_set_action_bar_state`](<#prv_set_action_bar_state>)

### prv\_skipping\_click\_config\_provider<!-- {{#callable:prv_skipping_click_config_provider}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/music_app.c#L638>)

Configures button click handlers for music playback controls.
- **Inputs**:
    - `context`: A pointer to the context data for the click configuration.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Subscribes single click handlers for `BUTTON_ID_UP` and `BUTTON_ID_DOWN` to `prv_skip_click_handler`.
    - Checks the playback state using `music_get_playback_state()`.
    - Subscribes the `BUTTON_ID_SELECT` to either `prv_play_pause_click_handler` or `prv_ellipsis_click_handler` based on the playback state.
    - Subscribes long click handlers for volume control buttons to their respective handlers.
- **Output**: No return value; the function sets up the click configuration for the action bar.


---
### prv\_volume\_click\_config\_provider<!-- {{#callable:prv_volume_click_config_provider}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/music_app.c#L654>)

Configures button click handlers for volume control and play/pause actions.
- **Inputs**:
    - `context`: A pointer to the context data, typically used to store application state.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Subscribes to single repeating click events for the `BUTTON_ID_UP` and `BUTTON_ID_DOWN` buttons to handle volume changes.
    - Subscribes to a single click event for the `BUTTON_ID_SELECT` button to handle play/pause actions.
- **Output**: No return value; the function sets up the click configuration for the action bar.

### prv\_update\_layout<!-- {{#callable:prv_update_layout}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/music_app.c#L662>)

Updates the layout of the music app UI based on progress reporting support.
- **Inputs**:
    - `data`: Pointer to a `MusicAppData` structure containing UI elements and state.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Checks if progress reporting is supported using `music_is_progress_reporting_supported()`.
    - Sets a boolean `hide_layer` to true if progress reporting is not supported.
    - Hides or shows the track position bar and related text layers based on the value of `hide_layer`.
- **Output**: No return value; the function modifies the visibility of UI layers.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`music_is_progress_reporting_supported`](<../../services/normal/music.c.md#music_is_progress_reporting_supported>)
    - [`layer_set_hidden`](<../../applib/ui/layer.c.md#layer_set_hidden>)

### prv\_unload\_no\_music\_window<!-- {{#callable:prv_unload_no_music_window}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/music_app.c#L670>)

Releases resources associated with the `MusicNoMusicWindow`.
- **Inputs**:
    - `window`: A pointer to a `Window` structure that represents the no music window.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Casts the `window` pointer to a `MusicNoMusicWindow` pointer.
    - Deinitializes the bitmap associated with the `music_window` using [`gbitmap_deinit`](<../../applib/graphics/gbitmap.c.md#gbitmap_deinit>).
    - Deinitializes the bitmap layer of the `music_window` using `bitmap_layer_deinit`.
    - Deinitializes the text layer of the `music_window` using [`text_layer_deinit`](<../../applib/ui/text_layer.c.md#text_layer_deinit>).
    - Frees all internationalization resources associated with `music_window` using `i18n_free_all`.
- **Output**: This function does not return a value.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`gbitmap_deinit`](<../../applib/graphics/gbitmap.c.md#gbitmap_deinit>)
    - [`text_layer_deinit`](<../../applib/ui/text_layer.c.md#text_layer_deinit>)

### prv\_handle\_no\_music\_back<!-- {{#callable:prv_handle_no_music_back}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/music_app.c#L678>)

Handles the back button click event by popping all windows from the application stack.
- **Inputs**:
    - `recognizer`: A reference to the click recognizer that detects the button click.
    - `context`: A pointer to user-defined data, which is not used in this function.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calls `app_window_stack_pop_all` with `true` to remove all windows from the stack and animate the transition.
- **Output**: No return value; the function performs an action on the application window stack.

### prv\_no\_music\_window\_click\_config<!-- {{#callable:prv_no_music_window_click_config}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/music_app.c#L682>)

Configures the click event for the no music window.
- **Inputs**:
    - `context`: A pointer to the context data for the click configuration.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calls `window_single_click_subscribe` to register a single click event for the back button.
    - Associates the click event with the `prv_handle_no_music_back` function.
- **Output**: No return value; the function sets up the click configuration for the no music window.

### prv\_create\_no\_music\_window<!-- {{#callable:prv_create_no_music_window}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/music_app.c#L686>)

Creates a window that displays a "No Music" message and image.
- **Inputs**:
    - `void`: No input arguments.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Allocates memory for a `MusicNoMusicWindow` structure.
    - Initializes the window with a specific name and background color.
    - Sets the window's unload handler to clean up resources when the window is closed.
    - Retrieves the size configuration for the music app.
    - Initializes a bitmap with a resource for the 'no music' image.
    - Creates a rectangle for positioning the bitmap and initializes the bitmap layer.
    - Initializes a text layer with a message prompting the user to start playback on their phone.
    - Adds the bitmap layer and text layer to the window's layer.
    - Sets the click configuration provider for handling user interactions.
- **Output**: Returns a pointer to the created `MusicNoMusicWindow`.
- **Functions Called**:

    - [`window_set_background_color`](<../../applib/ui/window.c.md#window_set_background_color>)

    - [`prv_config`](<#prv_config>)
    - [`gbitmap_init_with_resource`](<../../applib/graphics/gbitmap.c.md#gbitmap_init_with_resource>)

### prv\_push\_no\_music\_window<!-- {{#callable:prv_push_no_music_window}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/music_app.c#L720>)

Creates and pushes a 'no music' window if it does not already exist.
- **Inputs**:
    - `data`: Pointer to a `MusicAppData` structure that holds the application state.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Checks if the `no_music_window` field in `data` is already set.
    - If it is set, the function returns without doing anything.
    - If it is not set, it calls `prv_create_no_music_window()` to create a new window.
    - Then, it pushes the newly created window onto the application window stack.
- **Output**: No return value; the function modifies the state of the application by pushing a window.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`prv_create_no_music_window`](<#prv_create_no_music_window>)


---
### prv\_pop\_no\_music\_window<!-- {{#callable:prv_pop_no_music_window}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/music_app.c#L728>)

Removes the 'no music' window from the application if it exists.
- **Inputs**:
    - `data`: Pointer to a `MusicAppData` structure that holds the application state.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Checks if the `no_music_window` member of the `data` structure is not NULL.
    - If it is not NULL, removes the window from the application window stack.
    - Frees the memory allocated for the `no_music_window` structure.
    - Sets the `no_music_window` member to NULL.
- **Output**: The function does not return a value. It modifies the state of the application by removing the 'no music' window.

### prv\_update\_now\_playing<!-- {{#callable:prv_update_now_playing}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/music_app.c#L736>)

Updates the current track information and UI state in the music application.
- **Inputs**:
    - `data`: Pointer to a `MusicAppData` structure containing the current state and UI elements of the music application.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Sets the visibility of the track position bar based on whether progress reporting is supported.
    - Retrieves the current track title and artist using `music_get_now_playing`.
    - Checks if user action is needed to start playback on the phone and shows or hides the no music window accordingly.
    - Compares the new title and artist with the existing ones to determine if they have changed.
    - If the title or artist has changed, updates the buffers and triggers an animation if necessary.
    - Calls [`prv_update_layout`](<#prv_update_layout>) to refresh the UI layout.
- **Output**: No return value; updates the UI and internal state of the music application.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`layer_set_hidden`](<../../applib/ui/layer.c.md#layer_set_hidden>)
    - [`music_is_progress_reporting_supported`](<../../services/normal/music.c.md#music_is_progress_reporting_supported>)
    - [`music_needs_user_to_start_playback_on_phone`](<../../services/normal/music.c.md#music_needs_user_to_start_playback_on_phone>)
    - [`prv_push_no_music_window`](<#prv_push_no_music_window>)
    - [`prv_pop_no_music_window`](<#prv_pop_no_music_window>)
    - [`prv_trigger_track_change_animation`](<#prv_trigger_track_change_animation>)
    - [`prv_update_layout`](<#prv_update_layout>)


---
### prv\_copy\_time\_period<!-- {{#callable:prv_copy_time_period}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/music_app.c#L765>)

Copies a time period in seconds into a formatted string in a buffer.
- **Inputs**:
    - `buffer`: A pointer to a character array where the formatted time will be stored.
    - `n`: The size of the buffer to prevent overflow.
    - `period_s`: The time period in seconds to be converted into a formatted string.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calculates the number of hours, minutes, and seconds from the input seconds.
    - Uses `snprintf` to format the time into the buffer based on whether hours are present or not.
- **Output**: The function does not return a value; it writes the formatted time string directly to the provided buffer.


---
### prv\_update\_track\_progress<!-- {{#callable:prv_update_track_progress}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/music_app.c#L777>)

Updates the track progress and UI elements based on the current playback state.
- **Inputs**:
    - `data`: Pointer to a `MusicAppData` structure that holds the current state of the music application.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Checks if track position updates are paused; if so, exits the function.
    - Checks if progress reporting is supported; if not, sets the progress bar to 0.
    - Calculates the percentage of track position relative to track length.
    - Updates the progress bar with the calculated percentage.
    - Copies the current track position and length into buffers formatted as time periods.
- **Output**: No return value; updates the UI elements directly.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`music_is_progress_reporting_supported`](<../../services/normal/music.c.md#music_is_progress_reporting_supported>)
    - [`progress_layer_set_progress`](<../../applib/ui/progress_layer.c.md#progress_layer_set_progress>)
    - [`prv_copy_time_period`](<#prv_copy_time_period>)


---
### prv\_update\_pos<!-- {{#callable:prv_update_pos}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/music_app.c#L793>)

Updates the current track position and progress in the music application.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Retrieves user data from the application state using `app_state_get_user_data()`.
    - Calls `music_get_pos()` to get the current track position and length.
    - Calls `prv_update_track_progress()` to update the track progress display.
- **Output**: No return value; updates the state of the music application directly.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`music_get_pos`](<../../services/normal/music.c.md#music_get_pos>)
    - [`prv_update_track_progress`](<#prv_update_track_progress>)


---
### prv\_handle\_tick\_time<!-- {{#callable:prv_handle_tick_time}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/music_app.c#L799>)

Handles the tick time event to update the music playback position if music is currently playing.
- **Inputs**:
    - `time`: Pointer to a `struct tm` representing the current time.
    - `units_changed`: A value of type `TimeUnits` indicating which time units have changed.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Checks if the current playback state is `MusicPlayStatePlaying`.
    - If playing, calls the [`prv_update_pos`](<#prv_update_pos>) function to update the playback position.
- **Output**: No return value; updates the playback position if music is playing.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`prv_update_pos`](<#prv_update_pos>)


---
### prv\_set\_pos\_update\_timer<!-- {{#callable:prv_set_pos_update_timer}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/music_app.c#L805>)

Sets a timer to update the music playback position based on the current play state.
- **Inputs**:
    - `data`: Pointer to a `MusicAppData` structure that holds the application state.
    - `playstate`: Current state of music playback represented by the `MusicPlayState` enum.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Checks if progress reporting is supported using `music_is_progress_reporting_supported()`.
    - If not supported, the function returns immediately.
    - If `playstate` is `MusicPlayStatePlaying`, it subscribes to a timer that triggers every second using `tick_timer_service_subscribe()`.
    - For any other `playstate`, it unsubscribes from the timer using `tick_timer_service_unsubscribe()`.
- **Output**: No return value; the function modifies the timer subscription for updating the playback position.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`music_is_progress_reporting_supported`](<../../services/normal/music.c.md#music_is_progress_reporting_supported>)


---
### prv\_configure\_music\_text\_layer<!-- {{#callable:prv_configure_music_text_layer}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/music_app.c#L820>)

Configures a `TextLayer` with specified parameters for displaying music text.
- **Inputs**:
    - `text_layer`: Pointer to the `TextLayer` to be configured.
    - `text_buffer`: Pointer to a character array containing the text to display.
    - `rect`: Pointer to a [`GRect`](<../../applib/graphics/gtypes.h.md#GRect>) structure defining the bounds of the text layer.
    - `y_offset`: Integer value representing the vertical offset for the text layer.
    - `align`: Enumeration value of type `GTextAlignment` specifying the text alignment.
    - `font`: Font to be used for the text layer.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Initializes the `text_layer` with parameters such as bounds, text, font, colors, alignment, and overflow mode using [`text_layer_init_with_parameters`](<../../applib/ui/text_layer.c.md#text_layer_init_with_parameters>).
    - Sets the bounds of the `text_layer` using [`layer_set_bounds`](<../../applib/ui/layer.c.md#layer_set_bounds>), adjusting the y-coordinate by the `y_offset` value.
- **Output**: The function does not return a value; it modifies the state of the `text_layer` directly.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`text_layer_init_with_parameters`](<../../applib/ui/text_layer.c.md#text_layer_init_with_parameters>)
    - [`layer_set_bounds`](<../../applib/ui/layer.c.md#layer_set_bounds>)
    - [`GRect`](<../../applib/graphics/gtypes.h.md#GRect>)

[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/music_app.c#L829>)

Initializes the user interface components for the music application.
- **Inputs**:
    - `window`: A pointer to a `Window` structure that represents the UI window.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Retrieves user data associated with the `window`.

    - Defines the size of the `window` and alignment for text.
    - Configures and adds text layers for artist, title, position, and length.
    - Initializes and adds a cassette container layer.
    - Initializes and adds a progress layer for track position.
    - Initializes and adds an action bar and status bar layers.
    - Retrieves the current track position and length.
    - Updates the UI state and layout based on the current music state.
- **Output**: No return value; the function modifies the UI components of the music application.

### prv\_push\_window<!-- {{#callable:prv_push_window}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/music_app.c#L920>)

Initializes and displays the music window in the application.
- **Inputs**:
    - `data`: Pointer to a `MusicAppData` structure that holds the state and UI elements of the music application.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Creates a pointer to the `Window` structure from the `data` input.
    - Calls [`window_init`](<../../applib/ui/window.c.md#window_init>) to initialize the window with the name 'Music'.
    - Sets the user data of the window to the `data` pointer.
    - Sets the status bar icon for the window using [`window_set_status_bar_icon`](<../../applib/ui/window.c.md#window_set_status_bar_icon>).
    - Defines a boolean variable `animated` and sets it to true.
    - Pushes the window onto the application window stack with animation using `app_window_stack_push`.
    - Calls [`prv_init_ui`](<#prv_init_ui>) to initialize the user interface elements of the window.
- **Output**: No return value; the function modifies the state of the application by displaying the music window.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`window_init`](<../../applib/ui/window.c.md#window_init>)
    - [`window_set_user_data`](<../../applib/ui/window.c.md#window_set_user_data>)
    - [`window_set_status_bar_icon`](<../../applib/ui/window.c.md#window_set_status_bar_icon>)
    - [`prv_init_ui`](<#prv_init_ui>)

### prv\_music\_event\_handler<!-- {{#callable:prv_music_event_handler}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/music_app.c#L931>)

Handles music-related events and updates the UI accordingly.
- **Inputs**:
    - `event`: Pointer to a `PebbleEvent` structure that contains information about the media event.
    - `context`: Pointer to user-defined context data, typically used to access application state.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Retrieves the current user data using `app_state_get_user_data()`.
    - Switches on the `event->media.type` to determine the type of media event.
    - If the event type is `PebbleMediaEventTypeNowPlayingChanged`, calls `prv_update_now_playing(data)`.
    - If the event type is `PebbleMediaEventTypePlaybackStateChanged`, sets a position update timer and updates the UI state.
    - For event types related to volume, server connection, or track position changes, retrieves the current track position and updates the track progress and layout.
    - Returns from the function after handling each event type.
- **Output**: No return value; the function updates the UI and internal state based on the media event.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`prv_update_now_playing`](<#prv_update_now_playing>)
    - [`prv_set_pos_update_timer`](<#prv_set_pos_update_timer>)
    - [`prv_update_ui_state`](<#prv_update_ui_state>)
    - [`music_get_pos`](<../../services/normal/music.c.md#music_get_pos>)
    - [`prv_update_track_progress`](<#prv_update_track_progress>)
    - [`prv_update_layout`](<#prv_update_layout>)


---
### prv\_handle\_init<!-- {{#callable:prv_handle_init}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/music_app.c#L957>)

Initializes the music application by allocating data, setting event handlers, and configuring UI elements.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Allocates memory for `MusicAppData` and initializes it.
    - Sets the event handler for media events.
    - Initializes various icons and images used in the UI.
    - Subscribes to the event service for media events.
    - Pushes the main window of the music application onto the window stack.
    - Requests reduced latency for music playback.
    - Sets a timer for position updates based on the current playback state.
- **Output**: No return value; the function initializes the application state and UI.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`gbitmap_init_with_resource`](<../../applib/graphics/gbitmap.c.md#gbitmap_init_with_resource>)
    - [`event_service_client_subscribe`](<../../../../tests/stubs/stubs_event_service_client.h.md#event_service_client_subscribe>)
    - [`prv_push_window`](<#prv_push_window>)
    - [`music_request_reduced_latency`](<../../services/normal/music.c.md#music_request_reduced_latency>)
    - [`music_request_low_latency_for_period`](<../../services/normal/music.c.md#music_request_low_latency_for_period>)
    - [`prv_set_pos_update_timer`](<#prv_set_pos_update_timer>)

### prv\_handle\_deinit<!-- {{#callable:prv_handle_deinit}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/music_app.c#L996>)

Cleans up resources and deinitializes the music application.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calls [`music_request_reduced_latency`](<../../services/normal/music.c.md#music_request_reduced_latency>) with `false` to restore latency settings.
    - Retrieves user data using `app_state_get_user_data` and stores it in `data`.
    - Calls `i18n_free_all` to free internationalization resources associated with `data`.
- **Output**: The function does not return a value; it performs cleanup operations.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`music_request_reduced_latency`](<../../services/normal/music.c.md#music_request_reduced_latency>)

### prv\_main<!-- {{#callable:prv_main}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/music_app.c#L1005>)

Initializes the music application, runs the event loop, and cleans up resources.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calls `prv_handle_init()` to set up the application.
    - Enters the application event loop with `app_event_loop()`.
    - Calls `prv_handle_deinit()` to clean up resources after exiting the event loop.
- **Output**: No return value; the function manages application lifecycle events.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`prv_handle_init`](<#prv_handle_init>)
    - [`prv_handle_deinit`](<#prv_handle_deinit>)

### music\_app\_get\_info<!-- {{#callable:music_app_get_info}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/music_app.c#L1013>)

Provides information about the music application.
- **Inputs**:
    - `void`: This function does not take any input arguments.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Defines a static structure `s_app_info` of type `PebbleProcessMdSystem` to hold application metadata.
    - Initializes the `common` field with a pointer to the main function `prv_main` and a UUID.
    - Sets the application name using `i18n_noop` for localization.
    - Optionally sets an icon resource ID if the capability for app glances is available.
    - Returns a pointer to the `s_app_info` structure.
- **Output**: Returns a pointer to a constant `PebbleProcessMd` structure containing metadata about the music application.

### prv\_set\_action\_bar\_state<!-- {{#callable_declaration:prv_set_action_bar_state}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/music_app.c#L277>)

Sets the action bar state and updates the UI accordingly.
- **Description**: Use this function to change the state of the action bar in the music application. It updates the user interface to reflect the new state. This function must be called with a valid `MusicAppData` structure that has been properly initialized. The function assumes that the `data` parameter is not null and that the `state` parameter is a valid `ActionBarState` enumeration value.
- **Inputs**:
    - `data`: A pointer to a `MusicAppData` structure. This must not be null and should be initialized before calling this function. The caller retains ownership.
    - `state`: An `ActionBarState` enumeration value representing the desired state of the action bar. Must be a valid value from the `ActionBarState` enum.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`prv_set_action_bar_state`](<#prv_set_action_bar_state>)  (Implementation)

### prv\_trigger\_cassette\_icon\_switch<!-- {{#callable_declaration:prv_trigger_cassette_icon_switch}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/music_app.c#L279>)

Switches the cassette icon to a new bitmap with optional animation.
- **Description**: Use this function to change the cassette icon displayed in the music application. It can switch the icon immediately or with an animation, depending on the `animated` parameter. This function should be called when you need to update the icon to reflect a change in state or user action. Ensure that the `new_bitmap` is different from the current icon if `animated` is true, as the function will not animate if the new icon is the same as the current one.
- **Inputs**:
    - `new_bitmap`: A pointer to a `GBitmap` object representing the new icon to display. Must not be null. The function does not take ownership of the bitmap.
    - `animated`: A boolean value indicating whether the icon switch should be animated. If true, the function will animate the transition unless the new icon is the same as the current one.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`prv_trigger_cassette_icon_switch`](<#prv_trigger_cassette_icon_switch>)  (Implementation)

### prv\_update\_cassette\_icon<!-- {{#callable_declaration:prv_update_cassette_icon}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/music_app.c#L280>)

Updates the cassette icon based on playback state.
- **Description**: Use this function to update the cassette icon in the music application interface. It changes the icon to a pause image if the music is paused, or to a cassette image if the music is playing. This function should be called whenever the playback state changes to ensure the icon reflects the current state. It can animate the icon change if specified.
- **Inputs**:
    - `data`: A pointer to a `MusicAppData` structure. This must not be null and should contain valid image data for the cassette and pause icons.
    - `animated`: A boolean value indicating whether the icon change should be animated. If true, the icon change will be animated; if false, it will change instantly.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`prv_update_cassette_icon`](<#prv_update_cassette_icon>)  (Implementation)

### prv\_update\_track\_progress<!-- {{#callable_declaration:prv_update_track_progress}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/music_app.c#L365>)

Updates the track progress display.
- **Description**: Use this function to update the track progress display in the music application. It checks if track position updates are paused and if progress reporting is supported. If updates are not paused and reporting is supported, it calculates the progress percentage and updates the progress bar accordingly. It also updates the position and length buffers with the current track position and length in seconds. This function should be called when the track position or length changes.
- **Inputs**:
    - `data`: A pointer to a `MusicAppData` structure containing the current state of the music application. Must not be null. The function reads from and writes to this structure to update the track progress.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`prv_update_track_progress`](<#prv_update_track_progress>)  (Implementation)

### prv\_skipping\_click\_config\_provider<!-- {{#callable_declaration:prv_skipping_click_config_provider}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/music_app.c#L487>)

Configures button click handlers for music control.
- **Description**: Use this function to set up button click handlers for controlling music playback. It configures single and long click actions for the UP, DOWN, and SELECT buttons based on the current playback state. This function must be called within a valid UI context where button interactions are expected.
- **Inputs**:
    - `context`: A pointer to user-defined data that is passed to the click handlers. This parameter must not be null and should point to a valid data structure used within the click handlers.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`prv_skipping_click_config_provider`](<#prv_skipping_click_config_provider>)  (Implementation)


---
### prv\_volume\_click\_config\_provider<!-- {{#callable_declaration:prv_volume_click_config_provider}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/music_app.c#L488>)

Configures button click handlers for volume control and play/pause actions.
- **Description**: Use this function to set up the button click handlers for controlling volume and toggling play/pause in a music application. It subscribes the up and down buttons to repeat click handlers for volume adjustment and the select button to a single click handler for play/pause toggling. This function should be called when the application needs to handle these specific button interactions.
- **Inputs**:
    - `context`: A pointer to user-defined data that is passed to the click handlers. This parameter can be null if no context is needed.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`prv_volume_click_config_provider`](<#prv_volume_click_config_provider>)  (Implementation)
